page: Microsoft Azure E20as_v4 is an EASv4-series virtual machine configured with an AMD EPYC 7763 processor. This x86_64 architecture server features 20 dedicated vCPUs mapped from 10 physical cores with multithreading enabled, running at a clock speed of 3.525 GHz. The system is provisioned with 160.0 GB of RAM, yielding a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 8.0 GB per vCPU. Local storage consists of a 328 GB volume, and the instance is Premium Storage capable.

Benchmark data indicates strong single-core CPU performance, with stress-ng single-core tests placing in the top 25%. Multi-core CPU performance and PassMark CPU scores fall within the average range. Memory subsystem performance is notable, with the PassMark memory score ranking in the top 25%, although sequential read and write memory bandwidth benchmarks for large blocks remain average. Cryptographic and integer operations are supported by hardware-accelerated instruction sets including AES, VAES, SHA_NI, BMI2, and FMA.

Due to the lack of dedicated hardware accelerators, the server exhibits poor performance in large-scale machine learning tasks, with LLM inference speeds for 70B models rated as poor. However, the high memory density and strong single-core performance make this instance highly cost-efficient for memory-bound workloads that do not require GPU acceleration. It is well-suited for database operations, memory-intensive web serving, caching layers such as Redis, and general-purpose enterprise applications requiring a high RAM-to-vCPU ratio.
description: The Microsoft Azure E20as_v4 is an EASv4-series virtual machine powered by an AMD EPYC 7763 processor. Operating on the x86_64 architecture, it features 20 dedicated vCPUs across 10 physical cores with a clock speed of 3.525 GHz. The instance is equipped with 160.0 GB of RAM, providing a high 8.0 GB per vCPU memory density, alongside 328 GB of local storage. Benchmark results show strong single-core CPU performance and strong memory performance, while multi-core operations, database transactions, and memory bandwidth fall into average ranges. Lacking a GPU, it performs poorly on large language model inference. This memory-optimized profile is highly suited for memory-intensive workloads, database operations, and caching systems like Redis, offering a balanced, cost-efficient resource allocation for enterprise applications.
og_description: Microsoft Azure E20as_v4 is an EASv4 virtual machine featuring 20 dedicated AMD EPYC vCPUs, 160 GB of RAM, and 328 GB of local storage, optimized for memory-intensive enterprise workloads and databases.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure E20as_v4 is a memory-optimized VM with 20 AMD EPYC vCPUs, 160 GB RAM, and 328 GB local storage for databases and caching.
tagline: A memory-optimized virtual machine featuring dedicated AMD EPYC processors and high memory density for database and caching workloads.
bullet_points: Powered by an AMD EPYC 7763 processor with 20 dedicated vCPUs
Equipped with 160 GB of RAM and 328 GB of local storage
Strong single-core CPU and memory benchmark performance
Premium Storage capable for high-throughput disk operations
Ideal for databases, Redis caching, and memory-intensive applications
